Empty
Reuse

Street-turn logistics, in your pocket

A native app that lets coordinators raise a reuse request, upload container photos, track line approval, and pay — from the depot, not the desk.

The brief

The depot doesn't have a desk

Empty-container reuse — street turns — gets decided in yards and on the road, not at a workstation. The people raising and paying for these requests needed the full workflow on a phone.

The problem

Coordinators were juggling reuse requests over calls and paperwork: photographing containers, chasing line approval, and arranging payment — with no single, mobile view of what stage each request was at or what it was saving. Good reuses slipped because the loop lived across too many places.

The job

Put the whole loop in one app: a dashboard that proves the value (containers reused, fuel, CO₂ and revenue), a request list anyone can triage by status, a guided way to raise and edit requests with photos, and a clean path to pay — all sized for one thumb in the field.

PlatformNative mobile
DomainEmpty reuse · Street turn
CurrencyINR ₹
EdgeFuel · CO₂ · ₹ saved
The loop

Raise, prove, approve, pay

One pipeline, start to finish — every reuse moves through the same five stops, all on the phone.

01

Raise

A 4-step request — line, port, date, containers.

02

Prove

Upload container photos for ML review.

03

Approve

Track status to line approval.

04

Pay

Settle the street-turn charge + GST.

05

Done

Banked savings, logged on the dashboard.

01 / Dashboard

Open on the impact

The home screen leads with proof: containers reused and revenue earned beside the fuel, CO₂ and distance saved. A status donut breaks the whole portfolio down at a glance, and reuse-by-city shows where the action is.

Numbers that earn the scroll

Every metric on the dashboard does a job — half prove ROI to the business, half prove sustainability. Putting them first makes the app feel like a results tool, not a form.

  • Reused, fuel, CO₂, revenue, distance — the five tiles up top.
  • Total Analysis donut — 200 containers split across five statuses.
  • Reuse by city — Pune, Nashik, Vasai, Panvel, Morbi at a glance.
  • Quick actions and a Filters sheet to slice the view.
02 / The reuse loop

Triage the list, open the detail

The Empty Reuse list shows every request with its shipping line, container and a single colour-coded status — filterable in a tap. Open one and the detail leads with that trip's projected savings, the import/export pair, and three tabs that hold the full story.

03 / Raise a request

A four-step ask, not a wall of fields

Raising a reuse is broken into four small steps with a progress bar — general info, route, containers, photos — so a coordinator can complete it one-handed without losing their place. Editing reuses the same shapes.

04 / Pay & done

Container-wise charges, one tap to pay

Payment is itemised per container — street-turn charge plus GST — with the option to apply credit balance or generate a payment link to send on. Card and UPI are both first-class, and a success screen closes the loop.

System

One electric blue, one green promise

The app runs on a single saturated blue scale for the product and a reserved green for everything sustainability — savings, approvals, progress. Status is always a colour you can scan in a list.

A yard's-worth of work,
in a thumb's reach

The app's job was to make a complex, multi-party logistics loop feel like four taps and a photo — while quietly keeping score of the fuel, carbon and rupees each reuse saves.
